title: agropyron repens var. leersianum
intro: Agropyron repens var. leersianum, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Agropyron repens var. leersianum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, often used in traditional folk medicine and herbal remedies.
